DARRtrLL W. LARSENI.-r
Countv Counsel \ --
OFFICE^QF THE COUNTY COUNSEL
. itouNrv oF surrER
1160 Civic Center Boulcmrd
Yuba City, Crlifomio 95993
T.lcphonr (916) 741.7110
February 6, 19 91
Fair Political Practices
Commi s s ion
Legal Div i s ion
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807
RE: COSTA REZONING
Gentlemen:
We have spoken with Attorney John wallace of your Legal Division
concerning this matter.
Pursuant to Government Code section
831I4(b) and 2 CCR 18329, we are requesting on behalf of
Supervisor Peter Licari 
formal written advice concernj-ng the
foJ-lowing matter. Peter Licari is a Sutter County Supervisor and
his mailing address is 1160 Cj,vic Center Boulevard, Yuba City,
California 
95993.
He has authorized this office to make this
request for formal written advice on his behal'f .
Properties owned by Manual Costa Iocated in the unincorporated
community of Tierra Buena, northwest of Yuba City, 
have been
proposed for a general plan change and rezoning. Attached hereto
please find the staff report to the Planning Commission, minutes
of the Planning Commission consideration of lhe matter, and a
staff report to the Board of Supervisors on the same subject.
The site 
map associated with the staff report contains a slight
error in designation of the subject site which error we have
corrected.
The subject site has frontage on both Butte House
Road and Elmer Avenue. Supervisor Licari's property is 
located
to the north of lhe subject site, 
is designated on the site rnap
that we have provided to you and has frontage on Elmer Avenue.
It is approximately five acres in area and contains Mr. Licari's
resi-dence.
As you can see from the staff report to the Board of Supervisors,
Planning staff is recommending to the Board that rather than
rezoning the property of Mr. Costa to a commercial designation,
the suggestion of staff is that it 
be rezoned to multi-family
residential. 
Multi-family residential zoning would be consistent
with the medium density residential designation placed on the
property by the applicable general plan. 
Mr. Licari's property
is designated Iow density residential, in that same plan and is
presently zoned R-1. To the extent it is of interest to you, the
Sutter County Assessor informs this office 
that a 1.95 acre
parcel zoned for commerciaL purposes (C-2) Iocated on Butte House
Road just west of Redding Avenue and the raiLroad tracks sold for
RONALD S. ERICKSON
Assistant
JAMES SCANI,ON
JOSEPH P. CERULI-O
Deputies


Fair Political Practices
Commission
February 6, l-99I
Page Two
a land-only price of $3.23 per square foot. A 12,000 square foot
Iot sold somewhaE to the west of the first parcel at a price for
J-and only of $3.50 per square foot in May of 1990. 
A local
realtor 
has informed us that many residentia.L lots are available
for sale in Yuba City at S35,000 per lot.
The Sutter County Planning Department informs us that they would
view as the "neighborhood" in which the subject and Mr. Licari's
properties are located as being bounded by the railroad which is
shown in the Tierra Buena General, Plan on both the west and south
sides of the area in question, the city Iimits of Yuba City which
are just east of Blevj,n Road on the east, and Young Road and its
extensions on the north. 
The character of the neighborhood is
described by the Planning Department as rural residential. 
The
Pl,anning Department does not believe that there is necessarily a
correlation 
between land uses on Mr. Costa's property and those
on Supervisor Licarirs. 
This is because the General PIan has
already 
drawn a 
distinction 
between the two properties,
designating Mr. Costars for medium density residential uses and
Mr. Licarj,'s for Iow density residential use. With regard to the
financial 
impact on Supervisor Licarirs property of any of the
contempl-ated changes on Mr . 
Costa I s property , the Planni ng
Department is unable to reach any conclusions, believing that
evaluation ot any such financial 
impact would be speculative.
Should lv1r. Costa's proposal be approved and the land developed
for commercial offices' 
lhe traffic 
impacts would be primarily
confined to Butte House Road and the southerly portion of Elmer
Avenue. That is, additional traffic might be introduced on Butte
House Road near l'1r. CosEa's property and similar.Ly on Elmer Road
in the immediate vicinity of l,lr. Costars property. It wouLd not
be anticipated that traffic 
increases generated by Mr. Costars
property would travel northerly. 
Rather, it would be expected
that they would travel east and south, to and from Yuba City.
Supervisor Licari, of course, \.rould Like to know from you whether
he may participate in the discussions and decision to be made by
the Sutter County Board of Supervisors with regard to Mr. Costars
request. 
If 
we can be of further assistance, please do not
hesitate to call upon us.
Very truly yours,
C72
/W16/"-"
DARRELL W. LARSEN
SUTTER COUNTY COUNSEL
DWL:js
Enc Iosures
cc: Supervisor Peter Licari

sections.question_synthetic: May Supervisor Peter Licari participate in discussions and decisions regarding a proposed rezoning of nearby property owned by Manuel Costa, given that Licari's own property is adjacent to and could potentially be affected by the rezoning decision?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from County Counsel to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No formal question or conclusion sections are present in the document. The legal question is embedded in the closing paragraph. Document references Government Code section 83114(b) and 2 CCR 18329 regarding formal written advice request.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: May Supervisor Peter Licari participate in discussions and decisions regarding a proposed rezoning of nearby property owned by Manuel Costa, given that Licari's own property is adjacent to and could potentially be affected by the rezoning decision?

embedding.summary: Sutter County Counsel requests formal written advice from the FPPC on behalf of Supervisor Peter Licari regarding whether he may participate in Board of Supervisors discussions and decisions on a proposed general plan change and rezoning of nearby property owned by Manuel Costa. The letter details the properties' locations, characteristics, and potential impacts.
